title: PI(4,5)P2 Imaging Using a GFP Reporter in Living Cells
author: Mariam Alkandari
license_note: PMC full-text XML — Bio-protocol — CC-BY confirmed in article XML
materials: Fetal bovine serum (FBS) (Gibco, catalog number: A
Two-chamber Lab-Tek ® II chambered German coverslip system (Thermo Fisher Scientific, catalog number:
Eppendorf Research ® pipettes, 0.5–10 μL, 10–100 μL, 20–200 μL, 100–1,000 μL (Eppendorf, catalog number:

For cell counting, load 10 μL of cell suspension into each chamber of the dual-chambered counting slide and insert into the Bio-Rad automated cell counter, which automatically provides total cell concentration in cells/mL.

steps.title: PH-PLCD1-GFP plasmid (Addgene, plasmid #51407; map available at: https://www
steps.instruction: PH-PLCD1-GFP plasmid (Addgene, plasmid #51407; map available at: https://www.addgene.org/51407 ) Reagents

steps.title: Biosphere ® plus filter tip, 1000...
steps.instruction: Biosphere ® plus filter tip, 1000 μL, 200 μL, 100 μL, 10 μL (Sarstedt)

steps.title: Seed U2OS cells in 1 mL...
steps.instruction: Seed U2OS cells in 1 mL of complete media (DMEM supplemented with 10% FBS and 1% AA) in a 2-well chamber slide system at a density of 50,000 cells per well one day prior to transfection. Note: The size of each well in a 2-well chamber slide system is 4.2 cm 2 . If a different well format is used, volumes/concentrations may be upscaled or downscaled accordingly.

steps.title: On the day of transfection, change...
steps.instruction: On the day of transfection, change media to AA-free DMEM media supplemented with 10% FBS (1 mL per well).

steps.title: In a 1
steps.instruction: In a 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube, dilute PH-PLCD1-GFP DNA plasmid in Opti-MEM to achieve a concentration of 0.5 μg in 75 μL of Opti-MEM per well.

steps.title: In another 1
steps.instruction: In another 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube, dilute Lipofectamine 2000 in Opti-MEM to achieve a concentration of 3 μL of Lipofectamine 2000 in 75 μL of Opti-MEM per well.

steps.title: Add diluted PH-PLCD1-GFP DNA (0
steps.instruction: Add diluted PH-PLCD1-GFP DNA (0.5 μg in 75 μL of Opti-MEM) to diluted Lipofectamine 2000 (3 μL in 75 μL of Opti-MEM) and gently mix by pipetting. Incubate at room temperature for 20 min.

steps.title: Add 150 μL of PH-PLCD1-GFP DNA-Lipofectamine...
steps.instruction: Add 150 μL of PH-PLCD1-GFP DNA-Lipofectamine complex mixture dropwise to each well and incubate cells at 37 °C with 5% CO 2 for 24 h.

steps.title: Twenty-four hours post-transfection, change transfection media...
steps.instruction: Twenty-four hours post-transfection, change transfection media to fresh complete DMEM media with AA and incubate cells at 37 °C with 5% CO 2 for an additional 24 h to allow peak expression of the GFP reporter, consistent with the general transfection guidelines. The post-transfection time frame for optimal reporter expression can be optimized based on the cell type being studied.

steps.title: Following 48 h post-transfection, cells are ready for imaging
steps.instruction: Following 48 h post-transfection, cells are ready for imaging. The robust expression of the GFP-tagged reporter in over 80% of U2OS cells confirmed successful transfection. B. Imaging U2OS cells transfected with PH-PLCD1-GFP Images of U2OS cells transfected with PH-PLCD1-GFP are acquired using a Zeiss Axio Observer Z.1 spinning disk confocal microscope at 100× magnification in a temperature-controlled chamber maintained at 37 °C with 5% CO 2 and ambient O 2 and humidity following the steps described below. Notes:

steps.title: While not strictly required, a spinning...
steps.instruction: While not strictly required, a spinning disc confocal microscope was used for live cell Z-stack imaging due to its ability to capture rapid transient dynamics through faster acquisition and its lower phototoxicity.

steps.title: By using the lowest laser power...
steps.instruction: By using the lowest laser power necessary to acquire high-quality images, along with a spinning disk confocal system, photobleaching was minimized and not observed under the conditions used.

steps.title: Imaging was carried out over a...
steps.instruction: Imaging was carried out over a period of approximately 10–20 min, during which pH was preserved by using CO 2 -buffered imaging medium. No replenishment of medium was required during the acquisition period as the imaging time was kept short and environmental parameters (e.g., temperature and CO 2 levels) were well controlled.

steps.title: Set up the parameters for image acquisition as follows
steps.instruction: Set up the parameters for image acquisition as follows: laser intensity at 20%, exposure at 65 m/s, and EM gain at

steps.title: Select cells that are well defined...
steps.instruction: Select cells that are well defined and not in proximity to other cells within the microscope field. Note: These settings can be used as a starting point and may require adjustment to optimize the signal to background ratio based on cell type, reporter expression, and microscope configuration ( Figure 1 ). Figure

steps.title: Zeiss imaging software interface for adjusting image acquisition settings
steps.instruction: Zeiss imaging software interface for adjusting image acquisition settings. The full screen view (left) displays the overall interface, with a red square indicating the region that is magnified in the inset (right) to show laser intensity, exposure, and EM gain settings used for image acquisition.

steps.title: Once a cell has been selected,...
steps.instruction: Once a cell has been selected, acquire z-stack images using a 488 nm excitation wavelength and an emission filter at 517 nm while keeping a constant interval (0.36 μm) between each focal plane ( Figure 2 ). Note: Image acquisition may be carried out in a blinded manner to minimize bias. Figure

steps.title: Zeiss imaging software interface for adjusting Z-stack image acquisition settings
steps.instruction: Zeiss imaging software interface for adjusting Z-stack image acquisition settings. The full screen view (left) displays the overall interface, with a red square indicating the region that is magnified in the inset (right) to show Z-stack image acquisition settings.

steps.title: After acquiring the Z-stack image, display...
steps.instruction: After acquiring the Z-stack image, display the image in orthogonal (ortho) mode, position the intersection point at the center of the cell, and create an image from the view ( Figure 3 ). Figure

steps.title: Zeiss imaging software interface for an...
steps.instruction: Zeiss imaging software interface for an orthogonal view of the cell generated from the Z-stack. The full screen view (left) displays the overall interface, with a red square indicating the region that is magnified in the inset (right) to show the intersection point at the center of the cell to generate an orthogonal view.

steps.title: In the graphics section, uncheck the...
steps.instruction: In the graphics section, uncheck the visibility option to hide the lines and save the image in TIFF format ( Figure 4 ). Figure

steps.title: Zeiss imaging software interface for an...
steps.instruction: Zeiss imaging software interface for an orthogonal view of the cell generated from the Z-stack. The full screen view (left) displays the overall interface, with a red square indicating the region that is magnified in the inset (right) to demonstrate how to hide axis lines by unchecking the visibility option in the graphics section. C. Quantification of the plasma membrane to cytosol ratio of PH-PLCD1-GFP

steps.title: Open images in the FIJI ImageJ...
steps.instruction: Open images in the FIJI ImageJ software and convert them to grayscale (8-bit or 16-bit). Notes:

steps.title: Quantification by FIJI may be carried...
steps.instruction: Quantification by FIJI may be carried out in a blinded manner to minimize bias.

steps.title: If your image is too bright...
steps.instruction: If your image is too bright or too dark, you may need to adjust the contrast/brightness.

steps.title: Go to the ROI Manager by...
steps.instruction: Go to the ROI Manager by clicking Analyze > Tools > ROI manager .

steps.title: Select the rectangle tool from the...
steps.instruction: Select the rectangle tool from the Toolbar to draw the first square on the image. Note: Due to variability in cell size across cell types, the dimensions of the rectangular ROI were not strictly prespecified. Instead, the ROI size was adjusted to ensure appropriate sampling of either cytosolic or membrane signal, while maintaining consistency in size across ROIs within the same imaging field and across the same experiment. The ROI was chosen to be small enough to focus on subcellular regions of interest, rather than encompassing the entire cell.

steps.title: When the rectangle is in the...
steps.instruction: When the rectangle is in the desired position, click Add [t] in the ROI manager.

steps.title: Enable show all on the ROI...
steps.instruction: Enable show all on the ROI manager to view all saved ROIs.

steps.title: Draw three equal squares on the...
steps.instruction: Draw three equal squares on the dorsal plasma membrane (cell apex and immediately left and right) and another three identical squares below these in the cytosol, and three random squares outside the cell (background) ( Figure 5 ). Notes:

steps.title: For the squares positioned immediately to...
steps.instruction: For the squares positioned immediately to the left and right of the apex, place the squares at locations that encompass most of the membrane within each square.

steps.title: Membrane intensity was measured at the...
steps.instruction: Membrane intensity was measured at the apical region of the cell to standardize the sampling location. While the GFP signal may vary along the membrane, consistent apical sampling ensured comparability across conditions.

steps.title: A separate membrane marker was not...
steps.instruction: A separate membrane marker was not used, as the PH-PLCd1-GFP reporter provides sufficient and specific plasma membrane labeling due to its selective binding to PI(4,5)P 2 .

steps.title: Once the nine squares are added...
steps.instruction: Once the nine squares are added to the ROI manager, click measure in the ROI manager and export results ( Figure 5 ). Figure

steps.title: ImageJ interface showing placement of ROIs...
steps.instruction: ImageJ interface showing placement of ROIs corresponding to the plasma membrane, cytosol, and background. Mean fluorescence intensities were measured within each ROI to calculate the plasma membrane to cytosol intensity ratio.

steps.title: Calculate the mean intensity from the...
steps.instruction: Calculate the mean intensity from the three background ROIs and subtract this value from each of the individual mean intensity measurements for the plasma membrane and cytosol.

steps.title: Divide the mean intensity of each...
steps.instruction: Divide the mean intensity of each of the three squares on the dorsal plasma membrane (one on the apex and two immediately adjacent) by the mean intensity of the cytosolic ROI directly below it. The final plasma membrane to cytosol ratio of PH-PLCD1-GFP per cell is the average of three individual membrane to cytosol ratios. An Excel sheet with representative calculations is included in the supplementary materials (Supplementary file 1).

steps.title: By following steps C1–9, the plasma...
steps.instruction: By following steps C1–9, the plasma membrane to cytosol ratio of PH-PLCD1-GFP was quantified for two images to demonstrate the quantification approach described in this protocol ( Figure 6 ). Figure

steps.title: Quantification of PH-PLCD1-GFP localization
steps.instruction: Quantification of PH-PLCD1-GFP localization. A. acquired microscopy images of U2OS cells transfected with PH-PLCD1-GFP. B. Processed images from panel A using FIJI/ImageJ following steps outlined in section C to quantify plasma membrane to cystol fluorescence intensity ratio. C. Quantification dorsal plasma membrane: cytoplasmic PH-PLCD1-GFP ratio. Bars show mean± SEM. Statistical significance was determined using t-test, * P < 0.05.

description: Validation of protocol This protocol or parts of it has been used and validated in the following research article: Tavasoli et al. [23]. Defects in integrin complex formation promote CHKB -mediated muscular dystrophy. Life Science Alliance ( Figure 4A –C and G–I).
